purpose: Define the owner-visible macOS workbench layout rules for Source / Preview / Inspector without starting Phase 2.

defaultLaunchWindow.policy: Recommended balanced macOS workbench launch size when available display bounds allow it.

displayFitPolicy.policy: On smaller displays, use the largest centered window that fits within roughly 86% of available screen bounds while preserving a similar 16:10 feel.

comfortableWorkbenchWindow.policy: Comfortable owner-review and daily-workbench size; Source, Preview, and Inspector should all remain readable.

compactWorkbenchRange.policy: Compact observation range. At the minimum edge, right Inspector collapses first; left Source remains available unless the window drops into legacy stress coverage.

legacyStressViewport.policy: Legacy stress viewport only. It is not the default window, not the canonical macOS product size, not the main screenshot proof, and not the sole responsive acceptance criterion.

minimumSupportedWindow.policy: Preview Stage keeps priority; right Inspector collapses before primary playback controls become unreadable. 900x720 is retained only as a legacy stress viewport, not as the supported minimum.

fullWorkbench.policy: Left source, center preview, and right inspector are all inline and readable.

compactWorkbench.policy: Right inspector collapses first and may open as a drawer; left source remains inline.

minimalWorkbench.layout: LEFT rail | CENTER full | RIGHT overlay
minimalWorkbench.policy: Left source collapses to a rail after the right inspector; center preview remains primary.

layoutEngine.policy: Layout mode selection, panel widths, collapse priority, and transitions are resolved by the deterministic layout engine before CSS is applied.

regions.overflowRule: panel scrolls vertically; file names truncate with title text
regions.parentResponseRule: collapse to semantic strip before Preview Stage shrinks below minimum

regions.overflowRule: preview canvas scales; playback controls stay readable; metadata collapses first
regions.parentResponseRule: keeps visual anchor at default launch, comfortable, compact, minimum supported, and legacy stress sizes

regions.overflowRule: diagnostic copy wraps in detail surfaces; actions never masquerade as active future features
regions.parentResponseRule: collapse to semantic strip after source panel and before Preview Stage loses controls

components.wrapping: labels stay one line; values truncate with accessible full text
resource name truncates; badges never wrap vertically
layer name truncates; sprite attributes remain secondary
file identity/status/action/metadata/playback share one template across local, compare, and export-review
diagnostics wrap in cards; unavailable future actions stay review-only text
user-readable message first; internal diagnostics behind advanced disclosure
never vertical; truncate only at intentional compact icon threshold

responsiveRules.layout: source preview grid with inspector collapsed first
right inspector collapsed; source and preview remain usable
legacy stress viewport with side regions collapsed as needed

futureCapacityRules: Phase 2 optimization lives in Resources + Inspector Actions + Preview before/after; not visible as active P6-R1 buttons.
Phase 3 imageKey/text/key editing lives in Layers/Resources + Inspector Properties + Preview; not visible as active P6-R1 editing.
Phase 4 sequence repair lives in sequence resource groups + diagnostics + before/after preview; not implemented in P6-R1.
Multi-format and Agent/Motion Plan capacity must attach through adapters/jobs later, not through current clickable placeholders.
